scenario: i'm good , thanks . how are you ?
patient_utterances: [Clinician asked: "hey , miss christine , how are you doing today ?"] i'm good , thanks . how are you ?
[Clinician asked: "i'm pretty good . so it looks like you've completed the covid vaccine , that's great ."] yes , i did .

[Clinician asked: "anything new since your last visit ?"] no , i did all the tests that you had recommended me to take . i have n't been able to take the thyroid medicine , the one that you prescribed , as i'm still taking my old one . um , the price was a little high on the new one .
[Clinician asked: "okay , so did ... did you try the coupon that i gave you ?"] i did not try the coupon , uh , there was a charge of $ 75 .
[Clinician asked: "okay , well , next time that ... that coupon should help , and it should only be about $ 3 ."] okay , um ... i do n't have it , do you happen to have another one you can give me ?

[Clinician asked: "yep , right here ."] wonderful , thank you so much , and ... and then the gel , they are charging me $ 100 for it . so , i do n't know if this is because it's a ... it's wal-mart , or if i should try somewhere else , or ... maybe you know how or where i can get it cheaper .

[Clinician asked: "yeah , let's try something else , um ... sometimes it can be cheaper if we just prescribe you the individual ingredients of a medication , rather than the , the combined medication itself ."] that would be great .

[Clinician asked: "so , that's clindamycin gel and benzoyl peroxide , uh , maybe by doing them separately , they could be a lot cheaper . so , that we can do . the unithroid , with the discount code , should only be about $ 9 for 90 days ."] okay , that would be great . yeah , they were charging me $ 75 , and i just could n't pay that .
[Clinician asked: "maybe we'll try different pharmacy , as well ."] okay . so , do you think that my weight gain could have been the birth control that i was taking before that caused it ?

[Clinician asked: "maybe . i do n't really see an endocrine cause for it , at least , so i would need to see the , the hyperandrogynism or high testosterone . or , a high dhea , to cause acne , or hair growth , or any of that stuff . but , the numbers are n't showing up out of range ."] okay .

[Clinician asked: "i really do n't see any endocrine cause for it , like i said . your growth hormone was fine , but we definitely want to and need to treat it . um , i do n't know if we talked about maybe a little weight loss study ."] you mentioned the weight loss study , and you mentioned that i have some meal plans , um , that you had given me . i still have those , too .

[Clinician asked: "have you tried to make any changes in the diet since the last time we spoke ?"] i've been trying to get better . i will start back at the gym in july , because of my contract , i had to put a hold on it until then .
[Clinician asked: "okay ."] so , i do want to start doing that . i will be a little freer since , um , i'll be on vacation after july 8th .
[Clinician asked: "okay , good ."] and then my cousin was telling me to ask you about cla , because it's supposed to help your metabolism . is that okay to take ?
[Clinician asked: "um , i'm not sure . what is c , cla ?"] i'm not sure what it is , either .
[Clinician asked: "okay , well , i'm unfamiliar with it , so ..."] okay . i also have a coworker who has a thyroid issue too , and she suggested to try chromium for weight loss .

[Clinician asked: "so , that likely will not help too much . you can try either , if you really want to , but then ... it will not accept you into the weight loss study if you try those two ."] okay .
[Clinician asked: "chromium is just a supplement and it wo n't help that much ."] it wo n't , okay , thank you .

[Clinician asked: "it wo n't hurt ... okay , i should n't say that it wo n't hurt , but , it also wo n't help that much . so , it's up to you ."] okay . and so , my cousin also suggested amino acids , and that i might find them in certain foods , i guess , for my workout .

[Clinician asked: "yeah . amino acids are fine , they wo n't , wo n't really help with weight loss either , but it might help , uh , you replenish , and just kind of , feel hydrated ."] okay . are they proteins ? um , my cousin said she had lost some weight , and has been working out every day , but she does n't work , so ... i do n't know .

[Clinician asked: "yes , amino acids are what make up the protein , which is in any food you eat , with any protein . so , meats , dairy , nuts , any of that sort of thing ."] okay , thank you . got it .
[Clinician asked: "all right . um , are you allergic to any medications ?"] no , not that i know of .
[Clinician asked: "okay . is your s- skin pretty sensitive ?"] yes .
[Clinician asked: "all right ."] um , yeah , my size , i will start getting rashes , with different products .
[Clinician asked: "and have you ever tried clindamycin topical , as an antibiotic for your acne ?"] no , i've never tried anything for it .
[Clinician asked: "okay . we might give you some of that ."] okay . and i also want to mention that my feet do swell up a lot .
[Clinician asked: "okay . i'm ... let me take a look at that for just a moment . um , any constipation ?"] yes , i also do have that problem .
[Clinician asked: "all right . mira- miralax will definitely help with that ."] okay , yes , my doctor did also recommend that .
[Clinician asked: "great . all right , let's do an exam real quick . please have a seat on this table and i'll listen to your lungs and heart ."] okay .
[Clinician asked: "all right , deep breath . all right , again ."] okay .
[Clinician asked: "all right , sounds good ."] great .
[Clinician asked: "let me take a look at your feet and ankles ."] okay .
[Clinician asked: "all right , they look okay right now , certainly let your doctor know about this if it gets any worse or reoccurs ."] okay , i will do that .

[Clinician asked: "now , let's go over your lab work . so , when you took that pill , the dexamethasone test , you passed , which means you do n't have cushing's syndrome . on that test , at least . the salivary cortisol , though , unless you did one wrong ... two of them were completely normal and one was abnormal , "] okay , that's okay .

[Clinician asked: "all right , so , your cholesterol was quite high . the total cholesterol was 222 . the good cholesterol was about 44 . the bad was 153 , and it should be less than 100 . the non-hdl was about 178 , and it should be less than 130 . the good cholesterol should be over 50 , and it was 44 . so , your sc"] yes , we did . i- i do need to take one today , though .

[Clinician asked: "okay . so , i also checked a lot of other pituitary hormones , iron levels ... everything else seemed to be pretty good , and in decent range ."] okay , that sounds great . so , i wanted to also show you my liver enzymes , um , because i have n't come back since then ... but i was also happy , because one of them was back to normal .
[Clinician asked: "okay , great . let's see them ."] okay . so , the one that's 30 , that was almost 200 not so long ago .
[Clinician asked: "yeah , your alt was about 128 ."] okay , and , and back in october was 254 .
[Clinician asked: "yeah , this is much better ."] okay , great . and then it dropped in january , and then it dropped a little more in march , since i stopped taking the medicine in december .

[Clinician asked: "okay , that's good . so ... i'm proud of you with the course of your labs , so before i forget , i'm going to , uh , just put your labs into the computer today , and i wo n't be checking your vitamin d level for some time ."] okay . so , with the thyroid , and the low vitamin d , does that always happen together ?
[Clinician asked: "um , i do have a lot of people that have thyroid , thyroid issues and they have vitamin d deficiency ."] okay .

[Clinician asked: "this is what i'm , um , i'm going to do . i'm going to put , print out your prescriptions , so you can shop around at the pharmacies and see if you can find better prices ."] okay , that way i can go ask them and try cvs .
[Clinician asked: "yeah , that sounds like a plan ."] okay , good . so , the weight loss study that you mentioned , when does that start ? or , how does that work ?
[Clinician asked: "so , we are about to start , as we just got approval last week , and we are just waiting on our paperwork so we can get started ."] okay , and what's involved with that ?

[Clinician asked: "so , it'll involve you receiving a medication which has been used for diabetes treatment , and it works mostly in the gut on satiety , or satiety hormones . um , the most common side effects are going to be nausea , vomiting , diarrhea and constipation . they are s- uh , six arms , to the study . on"] okay .

[Clinician asked: "so , it's a way of getting them , but , the odds of getting one of the arms with the medication that are in your favor , right , might be only one out of five of our hundred patients that we have on the list for the study that will receive the placebo ."] okay .
[Clinician asked: "does that make sense ?"] yes , it does .

[Clinician asked: "so , we do expect pretty big weight loss , because of what we learn in diabetes study . so , it's a year long , uh , process , and it's an injection once a week . you come in weekly for the first four , five weeks , i believe . and then , after that , it's once a month . you do get a stipend for par"] okay , do you know how much the stipend is ?

[Clinician asked: "um , i will have to double check for you , and , you do n't have to be my patient , you just have to meet the criteria . so the criteria is a bmi greater than 30 , if you do n't have any other medical condition . or , a bmi greater than 27 , if you do have another medical condition , like your chole"] i have a friend who might be interested , and she does have diabetes .
[Clinician asked: "if she has d- diabetes , then she wo n't qualify ."] okay , you ca n't if you ... if you have diabetes , got it .
[Clinician asked: "correct . yeah , the only thing that , um , they can not have , really , is diabetes . so , either a psychiatric disease , or schizophrenia , bipolar , things like that ."] okay .
[Clinician asked: "but , if they have hypertension , high cholesterol , things like that ... they can definitely sign up ."] and they can , okay . thank you for explaining that .

[Clinician asked: "of course . so , do you want me to try to get you into that study ? or , would you just like to try , me to prescribe something ? it's kind of up to you ."] i think i'll just wait for a little bit now .

[Clinician asked: "all right , sounds good . i'll give you the information for the research , it's just in my office . um , it is a different phone number , though . so then , if you're interested , just call us within a month , because i do n't know how long , uh , the , the wait will be ."] okay , will do .

[Clinician asked: "perfect . so , let me go grab your discount card for the unithroid . um , when you go in to activate it , the instructions are on this card , and then you use your insurance ... then , show them this , and ask how much it'll cost . if it's too expensive , just let me know ."] i will . thank you so much for your help on that .

[Clinician asked: "you're welcome . then , what i did is , i gave you a topical antibiotic , plus i gave you the benzoyl peroxide . so , the peroxide may bleach your sheets , but , you want to make sure to take it and apply it at night , so you do n't have a reaction from the sun during the day ."] okay , i can do that .
[Clinician asked: "but , you do also want to make sure that you do n't mess up your sheets ."] okay , sounds good .

[Clinician asked: "um , so , that's that . and then , let's see how you do on the other medications . i think this will , this will get better . in the meantime , a low-carb diet , avoid alcohol and fatty foods , and low chole- cholesterol foods ."] okay .

[Clinician asked: "and again , once you finish your dose of vitamin d , for the vitamin d deficiency , you're gon na start with the 2000iu daily , so that you're able to maintain those levels . sound good ?"] yes , that sounds great .
[Clinician asked: "i really think your liver enzymes are going to get better once you lose the weight , though ."] okay , that would be great .
[Clinician asked: "since we stopped your birth control , we can try once called phexxi , which is kind of like a spermicide , basically ."] okay .
[Clinician asked: "and you just apply it before intercourse ."] okay .
[Clinician asked: "if you need some , uh , just let me know ."] okay , i will . i'll let you know .

[Clinician asked: "okay , perfect . so , stay put for me now . i'm going to go see if they have discount samples , and bring you that prescription . and then , i'm going to order the labs for next time ."] okay , great , thank you so much .
gold_note:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Hypothyroidism.
Abnormal liver enzymes.
Abnormal weight gain.
Acne vulgaris.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Christine Hernandez is a 39-year-old female who presents today for evaluation and management of abnormal liver enzymes and hypothyroidism.

When the patient was last seen, her thyroid medicine was changed to Unithroid, but due to the cost of $75, she has not yet started the new medicine and continues taking her previous thyroid medicine. She denies utilizing the coupon that was previously recommended for the Unithroid. Similarly, the patient was prescribed Epiduo for her acne vulgaris, which she did not fill due to the cost of $100. She tried filling both prescriptions at Walmart.

Mrs. Hernandez still presents with abnormal weight gain. She questioned if her oral contraceptive Microgestin, which was discontinued, could have been the cause. Additional symptoms included acne, hirsutism. She is attempting to modify her intake and plans to resume her gym membership in July as she will be on vacation. She inquired about the impact on weight loss of chromium, CLA, and amino acids.

The patient confirmed that she is taking her high dose Vitamin D as prescribed. She does suffer from constipation and she treats with MiraLAX.

She denies previous treatment for her acne vulgaris. She denies ever using clindamycin topical antibiotic. She reports having sensitive skin, developing rashes with various skin products.

PAST HISTORY

Medical
Hypothyroidism.
Acne Vulgaris.
Vitamin D deficiency.
Abnormal weight gain.
Hirsutism.
Constipation.

CURRENT MEDICATIONS

Benzoyl Peroxide.
Clindamycin Gel applied topically.
Vitamin D 2000 IU daily.
Unithroid.
MiraLAX.

ALLERGIES

No known allergies.

RESULTS

Dexamethasone Suppression Test: Normal.

Salivary Cortisol Tests: 2 out of 3 were normal. Not consistent with Cushing’s Syndrome.

Hepatic Function Panel: ALT 128.
Lipid Panel: Total cholesterol 222 mg/dL, HDL 44 mg/dL, LDL 153 mg/dL, non-HDL 178 mg/dL.

FAI: Normal.

ASSESSMENT

• Hypothyroidism.
• Abnormal liver enzymes.
• Abnormal weight gain.
• Acne vulgaris
• Vitamin D deficiency.
• Hirsutism

PLAN

Hypothyroidism
The patient will again attempt to initiate Unithroid. I have printed out the prescription and a discount card, which should result in a reduced price. She will contact my office if she encounters additional issues.

Abnormal liver enzymes
We have seen improvement since the discontinuation of Microgestin. I recommended the spermicide Phexxi for alternative birth control. The patient will also continue working on weight loss, which should additionally improve her liver enzymes.

Abnormal weight gain
Cushing’s Syndrome has been ruled out. I reviewed the weight loss study with the patient, she will call within a month if she is interested in participating. I advised her to follow a low carbohydrate, low cholesterol, abstain from alcohol, and reduce fatty foods diet.

Acne vulgaris
Epiduo was not covered and too expensive. The patient will try benzoyl peroxide and Clindamycin separately to see if the price improves.

Vitamin D deficiency
The patient will complete the high dose vitamin D and then should start vitamin D 2000 IU per day to maintain her levels.

Hirsutism
Her androgen levels were normal.

INSTRUCTIONS

She will repeat all labs prior to her next visit.

naive.response_text:
Thank you for sharing all of that with me. I can tell you're putting a lot of effort into managing your health, and I appreciate your openness. Let's work together to address these concerns and find solutions that fit your needs and budget.

For the medication costs, we have several options to explore, like using coupons, checking different pharmacies, or splitting prescriptions if possible. I'll help you find the most affordable solutions. Regarding your weight gain, we discussed the possible connection to your previous birth control, but lifestyle factors also play a role. I encourage you to continue with your gym plan and we'll monitor your progress. The weight loss study is a great option, and I'll provide more details so you can decide if it's right for you.

As for the supplements, while they aren't proven to aid weight loss, they're generally safe if you're interested in trying them. We'll focus on realistic expectations and effective strategies for your health goals. I'll also review your lab results with you to ensure everything is on track.

You're not alone in this journey, and I'm here to support you every step of the way. Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have more questions or need adjustments to your plan. Let's keep moving forward together.

strong_prompt.response_text:
Thank you for sharing all of this with me. It sounds like you're facing some challenges with medication costs and managing your health, which can be really overwhelming. I understand how frustrating it must be to deal with high prices, especially when trying to stay healthy. Let's work together to find solutions that fit your budget and needs.

We discussed the high cost of your thyroid medication and the gel. I know that financial barriers can make it hard to stick with treatment, so I suggested using coupons and trying different pharmacies to find the best price. I also mentioned the possibility of splitting your medication into cheaper generics if needed. For the gel, we can explore individual ingredients to see if that reduces the cost. I'll provide you with discount cards and information to help you shop around.

Regarding the weight loss study, it's a great option if you're interested. It involves a medication that helps with satiety and is given through injections. The study is open to those with a BMI over 30, and it includes regular follow-ups and a stipend. I can give you more details and help you get started if you're interested. It's a long-term commitment, but it could be very effective for weight management.

For your skin concerns, we've prescribed a topical antibiotic and benzoyl peroxide. Applying these at night can help avoid irritation from the sun. I'll also review your diet and lifestyle to support your skin health. Additionally, we'll monitor your liver enzymes and vitamin D levels to ensure everything is on track.

Please let me know if you have any questions or need further clarification on any of these steps. I'm here to support you and help you navigate these challenges. We'll check in again in a few weeks to see how things are going and adjust as needed. If you have any concerns before then, don't hesitate to reach out.

full.response_text:
Thank you for sharing your concerns about your acne and your goal of finding an affordable treatment. I understand how important it is to find a solution that works well for you without being too costly.

Based on the information we have, it seems that the treatments you've tried so far haven't fully addressed your acne. We'll need to explore other options together to find something that works better for you.

We want to make sure you have access to treatment options that are both effective and affordable. We'll discuss available options and work with you to find the best fit.

We'll review the treatment options available and discuss their costs to find something that fits your needs.

We'll schedule a follow-up to check on your progress and adjust the treatment plan as needed.

Can you tell me, in your own words, what we discussed today about your acne treatment options?

Let's plan to follow up in two weeks. If you notice any changes in your skin or have any questions before then, please reach out.
